Camptotheca acuminata, also known as Happy Tree or Camarotis adnata, is a deciduous tree native to China. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, anti-cancer, and anti-viral properties. The bioactive constituents of Camptotheca acuminata include camptothecin, 10-hydroxycamptothecin, and 9-aminocamptothecin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Camptotheca acuminata include extracts, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions, including cancer and inflammation.
